controls.name: LLM01: Prompt Injection
controls.description: Attackers manipulate LLMs via crafted inputs causing unintended actions or data disclosure. Prompt injection occurs when user-supplied data alters the intended instruction context.
controls.gap_diagnosis: No input guardrail is present to protect prompt nodes with injection risk.
controls.fix_guidance: Add an input validation guardrail covering all external prompt sources. Consider using a dedicated prompt injection detection library or LLM guard.

assertions.description: At least one GUARDRAIL node must exist to intercept injected inputs

controls.name: LLM02: Insecure Output Handling
controls.description: Downstream components blindly trust LLM output, enabling XSS, SSRF, privilege escalation, or remote code execution when output is passed to interpreters without sanitisation.
controls.gap_diagnosis: TOOL nodes are present but no output-validation GUARDRAIL was detected.
controls.fix_guidance: Implement an output validation guardrail that sanitises LLM responses before they are passed to downstream tools, interpreters, or APIs.

assertions.description: TOOL nodes exist (output is acted upon)
A GUARDRAIL must cover output before it reaches downstream tools

controls.name: LLM06: Sensitive Information Disclosure
controls.description: Insufficient safeguards allow LLMs to reveal confidential data, PII, PHI, trade secrets, or system credentials through direct inclusion in responses.
controls.gap_diagnosis: A DATASTORE with PII/PHI classification exists but no AUTH node protects access to it.
controls.fix_guidance: Add authentication controls on all datastores that contain classified data. Implement output filtering to redact sensitive fields before they reach the LLM context.

assertions.description: AUTH node must exist to gate access to datastores holding classified data
A GUARDRAIL for output filtering is recommended

controls.name: LLM08: Excessive Agency
controls.description: LLM-based agents are granted excessive permissions (file-write, code-execution, admin API calls) without adequate oversight, enabling broad impact from prompt injection or hallucination.
controls.gap_diagnosis: PRIVILEGE or TOOL nodes with high-risk scopes (db_write, code_execution) are present without a GUARDRAIL covering them.
controls.fix_guidance: Apply the principle of least privilege. Add guardrails for any action with write, execute, or destructive scope. Require HITL approval for high-risk operations.

assertions.description: A GUARDRAIL must exist to constrain high-privilege tool and agent actions

controls.name: LLM09: Overreliance
controls.description: Systems or people rely too heavily on LLM outputs without adequate verification, leading to security vulnerabilities, misinformation, and undetected hallucinations.
controls.gap_diagnosis: MODEL node is present but no GUARDRAIL for hallucination detection or output verification was found.
controls.fix_guidance: Implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) or a fact-checking guardrail. Add human review workflows for high-stakes outputs. Monitor model outputs for anomalies.

assertions.description: MODEL node must exist (control only applies when a model is in use)
A GUARDRAIL for output validation or hallucination mitigation must exist

controls.name: LLM10: Model Theft
controls.description: Attackers gain unauthorised access to proprietary LLMs through excessive API access, side-channel attacks, or direct exfiltration of model weights or fine-tuning data.
controls.gap_diagnosis: MODEL node is present but no AUTH node was found to protect access.
controls.fix_guidance: Restrict model API access using authentication and authorisation controls. Implement rate limiting on model endpoints. Monitor for unusual query volumes or adversarial probing patterns.

assertions.description: MODEL node must exist (control only applies when a model is in use)
AUTH node must exist to protect model API access
